scams.title: Friendship bracelet / rose scam
scams.where: Paris (Sacré-Cœur, Eiffel), Rome (Spanish Steps), Barcelona (Las Ramblas)
scams.what: Stranger ties bracelet on wrist or thrusts rose into hand, then aggressively demands €5-50 + scenes if you refuse
scams.avoid: Hands in pockets in tourist areas. Walk past, don't make eye contact. If tied: take it off, drop it, walk away.

scams.title: Petition scam (deaf-mute clipboard)
scams.where: Paris (Eiffel, Louvre, Trocadéro), Rome, Berlin Brandenburg, Barcelona
scams.what: Group of 'deaf-mute' women/children with clipboard distract you for petition signature; accomplice picks pocket
scams.avoid: Hands on bag/pockets when approached. 'Non!' / 'No!' firmly. Move away. Real charities don't street-collect.

scams.title: Gold ring 'find'
scams.where: Paris, Rome, Barcelona near tourist sites
scams.what: Person 'finds' gold ring, offers to sell it cheap (€20). Ring is brass.
scams.avoid: If 'found' near you, walk away. Don't engage.

scams.title: Bird poop / mustard / spilled drink
scams.where: Rome, Barcelona, Paris, London tourist hubs
scams.what: Substance lands on you; helpful stranger offers to clean while accomplice picks pocket
scams.avoid: Politely refuse help. Move to a café/store before cleaning. Don't put bag down.

scams.title: Metro pickpocketing (organized gang)
scams.where: Paris Métro Line 1+4 (CDG-Châtelet); Rome Metro Line A (Termini-Vatican); Barcelona L3 + L4
scams.what: Children + adult teams crowd you at door, jostle, lift wallet/phone. Highest rate at Eiffel, Colosseum, Sagrada Familia stops.
scams.avoid: Crossbody bag IN FRONT, hand on it. Phone in zipped pocket NEVER back pocket. Stay aware near doors at stops.

scams.title: Fake police 'wallet check'
scams.what: Plainclothes 'police' demand passport/wallet for 'counterfeit money check'. Take cash + cards.
scams.avoid: Real police NEVER ask for wallet on the street. Demand to walk to nearest police station. Walk away if they don't agree.

scams.title: Restaurant menu-swap / no-prices
scams.where: Rome (Trastevere, near Trevi), Paris (near Notre-Dame, Champs-Élysées)
scams.what: Verbal 'fish of day' or untranslated menu = €100+ shock bill. 'Coperto' (cover charge) added with stiff tip
scams.avoid: Read menu prices before sitting. Ask 'how much?' for any verbal special. Avoid restaurants without prices on menu.

scams.title: Gladiator photo scam (Rome)
scams.what: Costumed 'gladiators' pose for photo; demand €20-50 after
scams.avoid: Don't pose with them. If they appear in your shot, walk on without paying.

scams.title: Taxi airport overcharge
scams.where: All major airports — Rome FCO, Paris CDG, Madrid Barajas, Barcelona BCN, Berlin BER
scams.what: Driver claims 'flat rate' or no meter; €100+ for €40 ride
scams.avoid: Use Uber/Bolt/FreeNow apps. OR insist on metered taxi from official taxi rank only. Rome + Paris have fixed flat-fares posted online (Rome FCO €50).

scams.title: ATM skimming (multiple countries)
scams.where: Standalone ATMs in tourist areas — especially Italy, Spain, Berlin
scams.what: Skimming device + camera; account drained within hours
scams.avoid: Use ATMs INSIDE bank branches. Cover PIN entry. Use credit not debit (better fraud protection).
